# Questions in Livestream 7

Live Stream #7: Seven and the Ragged Tiger

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15</a> | Did Psusennes I get the silver for his coffin by reusing older royal material, or from trade? | He thinks it likely came through trade or mining supply rather than reuse; silver was precious, harder to work than gold, and its use in Third Intermediate Period royal burials signaled wealth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=302" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:02</a> | What is your opinion about the Ezekiel 29-30 prophecy about Nebuchadnezzar plundering Egypt and leaving it desolate? Is there archaeological evidence? | He says there is evidence consistent with Nebuchadnezzar reaching Elephantine and plundering sacred treasures, after which Egyptian sacred barks and treasures appear more diminished. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:40</a> | Is the mytho-history interpretation of Genesis 1-11 plausible, as endorsed by Gordon Wenham, Richard Averbeck, and William Lane Craig? | He agrees it is plausible: Genesis has historical aspects but is also mythologized and analogized in a way that fits ancient Near Eastern context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=541" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:01</a> | What do you make of Graham Hancock-style ancient timelines for Egypt? | He strongly rejects them, using the Sphinx and Giza limestone erosion as examples; he argues wind erosion over thousands of years explains the evidence better than a 12,000-year-old chronology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:56</a> | What are good references for ancient Egyptian cuisine? | He mentions an ancient Egyptian herbal as the source that comes to mind, but says no specific cuisine book immediately comes to mind. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:57</a> | What is the earliest text for the Opening of the Mouth ritual in the context of statues and the dead? | He believes the earliest reference is in the Pyramid Texts, in the Sixth Dynasty, and explains the rite as enabling the deceased's spirit and offerings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=866" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:26</a> | If Thutmose III was the pharaoh of Joseph and had a temple of Amun at Karnak, why would a Hyksos king have a temple at Karnak? | He rejects the premise: Thutmose III was not Joseph's pharaoh, the Hyksos did not conquer Thebes, and the identification makes no sense to him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:03</a> | Did Egyptians have real magic as seen in the plague narrative? | As a historian he does not answer whether real magic exists, but says Egyptians practiced magic and sometimes used tricks or rigged statues; whether there was actual magical power is impossible for him to answer historically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=1134" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:54</a> | What is the earliest record of Canaanite languages that we have? | He points to an unclear Syrian discovery name in the transcript, around 2350-2400 BC, while noting the language is not yet fully translated and the identification remains cautious. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=1242" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:42</a> | Can you explain the "submarine and helicopter" hieroglyphs? | He identifies them as a palimpsest or superimposed writing at Dendera: one inscription was carved over another, creating the visual effect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=1393" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:13</a> | What is your opinion of the hypothesis that only the Levites experienced an exodus from Egypt? | He says Friedman's Levite exodus hypothesis depends on the Documentary Hypothesis, which he rejects, so he thinks the Levite exodus loses its basis and does not hold up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=1554" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:54</a> | Plato mentioned Egyptian references to Atlantis. Is there any truth or corroboration? | He says there is no solid corroboration; suggestions such as Santorini remain speculative, and at present Atlantis is only a story. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=1663" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:43</a> | Will we ever be able to know how Egyptians pronounced their language? | He says Coptic gives a fairly good but incomplete guide; consonants are often clearer than vowels, and some signs remain disputed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=1846" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:46</a> | What advice do you have for studying Egyptian through books or websites? | He recommends university study if possible, notes his own introductory videos, and names Collier, Hoch, and Gardiner's Egyptian Grammar as useful resources, though Gardiner is hard to obtain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2020" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:40</a> | What religious papyri or texts should I read beyond the Book of the Dead, Coffin Texts, Pyramid Texts, and Sinuhe? | He says there are many religious texts, including stelae, wisdom texts, Papyrus Westcar, Karnak temple inscriptions, Thutmose III's annals, and anthologies by Lichtheim or Simpson. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:47</a> | What is the story behind the Jews at Elephantine, and how were they distinct from their brethren? | He says they were Israelite refugees from Nebuchadnezzar who became mercenaries in Egypt, maintained Yahweh worship but also adopted other practices, kept ties to Canaan, and survived as a colony until roughly the first century BC. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2489" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:29</a> | Jericho had grain still in storage and findings that accord with the biblical account; is that true? | He says charred grain is present at the Middle Bronze destruction of Jericho, but charred grain is common at burned sites and is not by itself remarkable evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2588" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:08</a> | Have any of Champollion's works been translated into English? | He says many major works have been translated, though not all journals, and some are freely available as PDFs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:28</a> | Have you seen the Masonic temple in Washington, DC, that looks like an Egyptian temple? | He has not seen it, though he notes Masonic use of Egyptian symbolism and recalls visiting DC for an Egyptology conference and a National Geographic queens of Egypt exhibit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:38</a> | In Ezekiel, is the mentioned Daniel the biblical Daniel or a figure from another ancient tale? | He does not answer firmly because he has not studied Daniel closely enough; he notes there is another figure called Daniel and says he needs more study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=2834" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:14</a> | Would Moses' mother living at Avaris with Israel mean the pharaoh who adopted Moses lived in the eastern Delta, and would that disqualify late 18th Dynasty pharaohs? | He says no; Moses' adoptive mother would have belonged to the Ramesside clan as regional governors before Ramesses I, and late 18th Dynasty residence patterns alone do not disqualify them, though other data may. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:04</a> | If you could have the guitar of your dreams, what would it be? | His tastes change, but he mentions a Les Paul, Telecaster, and especially a Squier five-string bass as appealing, while noting he tends toward budget guitars. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3143" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:23</a> | Is the new synchronism about Terah connected to Zimri-Lim? | Yes. He says he will do a full episode on it, has checked the Mari source texts, and thinks it may support a northern Mesopotamian Ur rather than Ur in Sumer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3278" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:38</a> | Who is the best captain of the Enterprise, and why is it Robert April? | He says he prefers Benjamin Sisko over the Enterprise captains and was more into Deep Space Nine than the Enterprise series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3423" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:03</a> | What is your opinion on FARMS, the Mormon apologetic group at BYU, arguing that the Book of Mormon is an ancient document? | He rejects the claim, saying the Book of Mormon is not an ancient Near Eastern document and, even by its own claims, would be a New World document. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3652" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:52</a> | How good are James Hoffmeier's yellow and green books on Israel in Egypt and Sinai, and do you disagree with them? | He says both are generally good; the yellow Israel in Egypt book is approachable but out of date and getting a second edition, while the green Sinai book is more technical and not for beginners. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3749" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:29</a> | How can you tell if a king was Hyksos or an Egyptian pharaoh? | He points to name elements such as Seth associations in Delta or Hyksos contexts, inclusion or absence in Egyptian king lists, and process-of-elimination evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=3941" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:41</a> | Are there accounts of ancient Egyptians finding fossils or dinosaurs and incorporating them into myths or religion? | He says not really for Egypt, which lacks large dinosaur-type fossil deposits, though small marine fossils occur in Giza limestone; he contrasts this with other regions and mentions fossil shark teeth found in an Israelite context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:14</a> | What would be good reading material for Joshua's campaign? | He recommends Richard Hess and the volume Behind the Scenes of the Old Testament, edited by Greer, Hilber, and Walton, as good starting points. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4302" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:42</a> | Is Seven and the Ragged Tiger the name of a Duran Duran album? | Yes. He says it was their third album and notes his fondness for Duran Duran and 1980s music. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4399" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:19</a> | Are there problems translating bytdwd as "House of David" in the Tel Dan Stele? | He sees no problem with the translation, says alternative etymologies strain the evidence, and emphasizes that the stele was found in situ, supporting authenticity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:36</a> | What do you think of the idea that Tutankhamun's sisters ruled or co-ruled before or during his reign? | He is doubtful and does not find the evidence compelling, though he acknowledges Amarna chronology has ambiguities and says stronger evidence could change his view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:07</a> | What about claims that the Tel Dan Stele says "kettle" rather than "House of David"? | He treats such readings as overstrained attempts to avoid the House of David reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4762" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:22</a> | What do you make of the scholarly view that Daniel was written in the second century, at least in parts? | He is skeptical but says he is not yet prepared to address Daniel's authorship in detail and needs more reading before giving a full answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=4859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:59</a> | What do you think of the statement in Tom Holland's Dominion that Moses is never mentioned in Egyptian sources? | He calls it an argument from absence and says preservation problems, especially for royal and Delta records, mean lack of Egyptian mention cannot bear that conclusion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=5025" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:45</a> | Would you create a Discord server? | He says maybe, but he does not know Discord or its maintenance burden and asks viewers to explain how it would help. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=5130" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:30</a> | Was Avaris ever called Goshen, or something similar, by extrabiblical sources, or was Goshen purely a Hebrew name? | He says Avaris itself was not called Goshen by Egyptians as far as we know; Goshen is a regional designation in Hebrew texts, though new evidence could always change that. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=5219" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:59</a> | Could the plagues have been a principal cause of Egyptian domestic trouble in the late 13th and early 12th centuries? | He says it is possible but hard to prove; there was upheaval and weakening after Merneptah, but evidence is insufficient to say whether the plagues were a principal cause or an exacerbating factor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=5436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:36</a> | A man offered me a piece of the Ark of the Covenant for $2 million. Should I buy it? | No; he jokes that the viewer may already be wearing a piece of it and recommends his book on the Ark's Egyptian context instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=5526" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:06</a> | Was Akhenaten's religious reform a reaction to the Exodus? | He says no, because Akhenaten's reforms preceded the Exodus in his model, but he sees those reforms as part of the religious soil from which later Israelite monotheism emerged. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/99jbuPoy8_U?t=5638" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:58</a> | Any chance of a response video to Digital Hammurabi's series on Israelite slavery? | Maybe. He has not watched the series yet and says it belongs in the "maybe" bucket depending on other factors. |
